title: Even and odd functions: Graphs and tables
description: Given the graph of a function, determine if it's even, odd, or neither.
topic_path: Even and odd functions: Graphs and tables

assessment_items.question_content:
Function $f$ is graphed.

[[☃ image 1]]

**According to the graph, is $f$ even, odd, or neither?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###Definitions 

A function $f$ is even when $f(-x)=f(x)$ **for all** values of $x$.

Graphically, this means that if we reflect the function across the $y$-axis, the graph will stay the same.

A function $f$ is odd when $f(-x)=-f(x)$ **for all** values of $x$.

Graphically, this means that if we reflect the function across the $y$-axis and then across the $x$-axis, the graph will stay the same. [[☃ explanation 1]]

###Verification

We can see that the graph is symmetric about the $y$-axis, which you can verify for yourself by dragging the point on the $x$-axis from right to left. Therefore, $f$ is even.

[[☃ interaction 1]]
According to the graph, $f$ is even.

options.alt: A function that is symmetric across x equals zero on an x y coordinate plane. As x goes to negative infinity, the y value goes to negative infinity. As x goes to infinity, the y value goes to negative infinity. It passes through points (negative three and a half, zero), (negative two, six), (zero, zero), (two, six), and (three and a half, zero).

options.alt: A symmetrical graph over the line x equals 0 on an x-y coordinate plane. The x- and y- axes scale by one. The graph labeled f. It curves down from left to right from infinity passing through (negative three, five) and touches a point around (negative two, negative one). It curves up and back down around (negative one and a half, negative one half). It touches a point around (negative one half, negative four). It curves up to a middle point of the graph at (zero, negative two) and curves down to (one half, negative four). It curves up to (one and one half, negative one half) and curves down to a point around (two, negative one) before curving up to the point (three, five) and up towards infinity.

assessment_items.question_content:
Function $f$ is graphed.


[[☃ image 1]]

**According to the graph, is $f$ even, odd, or neither?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###Definitions 

A function $f$ is even when $f(-x)=f(x)$ **for all** values of $x$.

Graphically, this means that if we reflect the function across the $y$-axis, the graph will stay the same.

A function $f$ is odd when $f(-x)=-f(x)$ **for all** values of $x$.

Graphically, this means that if we reflect the function across the $y$-axis and then across the $x$-axis, the graph will stay the same. [[☃ explanation 1]]

###Verification

Reflecting the curve about the $y$-axis **and** the $x$-axis gives back the same curve, which you can verify for yourself by dragging the point on the $x$-axis from right to left and by dragging the point on the $y$-axis from top to bottom. Therefore, $f$ is odd.

[[☃ interaction 1]]
According to the graph, $f$ is odd.

options.alt: A function labeled f that is symmetric with respect to the origin on an x y coordinate plane. As x goes to negative infinity, the y value goes to negative infinity. As x goes to infinity, the y value goes to infinity.

A function $f$ is odd when $f(-x)=-f(x)$ **for all** values of $x$.

options.alt: A symmetrical graph labeled f about the origin on an x-y coordinate plane. The x- and y- axes scale by one. It curves down from left to right from infinity passing through (negative three, eight) and touches a point around (negative two, negative four). It curves up and back down through the midpoint at (zero, zero). It curves down and back up to (two, four), It then curves down passing through (three, negative eight) going towards negative infinity.

[[☃ image 1]]

**According to the graph, is $f$ even, odd, or neither?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###Definitions 

A function $f$ is even when $f(-x)=f(x)$ **for all** values of $x$.

Graphically, this means that if we reflect the function across the $y$-axis, the graph will stay the same.

A function $f$ is odd when $f(-x)=-f(x)$ **for all** values of $x$.

Graphically, this means that if we reflect the function across the $y$-axis and then across the $x$-axis, the graph will stay the same. [[☃ explanation 1]]

###Verification

Reflecting the curve about the $y$-axis **and** the $x$-axis gives back the same curve, which you can verify for yourself by dragging the point on the $x$-axis from right to left and by dragging the point on the $y$-axis from top to bottom. Therefore, $f$ is odd.

assessment_items.question_content:
Function $f$ is graphed.

[[☃ image 1]]

**According to the graph, is $f$ even, odd, or neither?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###Definitions 

A function $f$ is even when $f(-x)=f(x)$ **for all** values of $x$.

Graphically, this means that if we reflect the function across the $y$-axis, the graph will stay the same.

A function $f$ is odd when $f(-x)=-f(x)$ **for all** values of $x$.

Graphically, this means that if we reflect the function across the $y$-axis and then across the $x$-axis, the graph will stay the same. [[☃ explanation 1]]

###Verification

Reflecting the curve about the $y$-axis doesn't give back the original curve, which you can verify for yourself by dragging the point on the $x$-axis from right to left. So $f$ is not even.

Similarly, reflecting the curve about the $y$-axis **and** the $x$-axis doesn't give back the original curve, which you can verify for yourself by dragging the point on the $x$-axis from right to left and by dragging the point on the $y$-axis from top to bottom. So $f$ is not odd.

[[☃ interaction 1]]
According to the graph, $f$ is neither even nor odd.

options.alt: A function labeled f that is not symmetric on an x y coordinate plane. As x goes to negative infinity, the y value goes to negative infinity. As x goes to infinity, the y value goes to negative infinity.

options.alt: An absolute value function on an x y coordinate plane. Its vertex is at (negative three, zero). The graph starts at (negative eight, eight) and slants down from left to right to the vertex. The graph then slants up from left to right to a point at (five, eight).

assessment_items.question_content:
This table defines function $f$:

$x$ | $-2$ | $-1$ | $0$ | $1$ | $2$
:- | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$f(x)$ | $4$ | $0$ | $2$ | $0$ | $4$

**According to the table, is $f$ even, odd, or neither?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###Definitions 

A function $f$ is even when $f(-x)=f(x)$ for all values of $x$.

A function $f$ is odd when $f(-x)=-f(x)$ for all values of $x$.
We see that $f(-x)=f(x)$ for all values of $x$, so $f$ is even.

assessment_items.question_content:
This table defines function $f$:

$x$ | $-2$ | $-1$ | $0$ | $1$ | $2$
:- | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$f(x)$ | $2$ | $3$ | $0$ | $2$ | $3$

**According to the table, is $f$ even, odd, or neither?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###Definitions 

A function $f$ is even when $f(-x)=f(x)$ for all values of $x$.

A function $f$ is odd when $f(-x)=-f(x)$ for all values of $x$.
We see that $f(-x)\neq f(x)$ and $f(-x) \neq -f(x)$, so $f$ is neither even or odd.
